channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 55 replies — 90.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:39 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62377200
>>62372468
Nft lol

62377213
>>62372915
What do you mean?

62377217
>>62374451
How do you do that?

62377221
Ethereum

62377225
>>62372419
Link is fine. Wait till $1000

62377236
>>62371718
Not a BTC investor myself, but I imagine it's hard to ignore something that has repeatedly survived the kinds of events many thought would kill it.

62377444
Nothing about bitcoin has fundamentally changed.

62377533
>>62377444
C-checked

62377691
>>62377444
>Nothing about bitcoin has fundamentally changed.
checked and confirmed

62378619
>>62377217
You can natively stake it through a protocol while still maintaining self-custody. That way, you can earn more instead of just holding your BTC.

62378621
>>62372059
Maybe he's wrong. Maybe he's right. Either way, locking in gains after buying near the lows is hard to criticize.

62378868
>>62378619
If you're doing on centralised services you're cooked

62378871
>>62377691
Genius nerd. pretentious

62378977
>>62377225
go see a doctor

62379055
>>62377221
Long or short?

62379100
>>62373685
inflation is real

62380423
>>62378868
I didn't say that. Check my comment again. And if you look into TBVs, you'll probably get a better sense of what I mean.

62380435
>>62372189
You bullish or not?

62380445
>>62378868
This bot talks about bitcoin staking in every conversation on the subject, I concluded that it was a scam and should be avoided like the plague.

62380727
>>62372915
>Attack Bitcoin with 51% and try to break the chain
>Devs implement a temporary reversible limit to increase attacker costs
"Boohoo now we can't afford to attack it" kiss Bitcoin's ass ya bitch.

62381254
>>62380727
>>>>fuck off to other sha function, or mergemine, retards
>>>NO!
>>ok fine
>>*gets 99% of sha256 hashrete*
>omg they got 99% of sha256 hashrate!!!! quick, lets give up on PoW!!! we are the real BTC btc
kek

62381740
>>62381254
>Nigger can't even into PoW
Why would Bitcoin need to change it's PoW? Tell me where in the whitepaper this is suggested as a means to defend the original chain from a malicious scam fork.
>The real BTC
BTC doesn't even have a whitepaper and certainly cannot claim relation to the software described by Satoshi.

62382616
>>62379055
Your choice lol

62382799
>>62381254
You mean Bitcoin doesn't have any usecases?

62382804
>>62381740
Are you serious nigger?

62382895
buying when boomers capitulate and then riding it all the way to the top must be one of the best feelings ever, but somehow i always end up not buying because I always get lured by bears into waiting for "bettery entry prices".

62383146
>>62382799
Not really. Being a store of value is already a use case. Some people are just interested in whether BTC can be used for more than buying, holding, and eventually selling.

62383366
>>62383146
Where do you do that?

62383375
>>62382895
Just buy today and you will thank in 10 years

62383385
>>62377444
it has

item.title: I talked to two people today about rent. — 157 replies — 61.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I talked to two people today about rent.

Open thread • Replies: 157 • Updated: 2026-06-18 06:42 UTC

OP

I talked to two people today about rent.

The first guy said he lives in Atlanta and pays about $2,300 a month in rent. I mentioned that works out to roughly $27,600 a year, which I personally think is a lot for rent. He also told me he was let go from a sales job at a small company about two weeks ago. Small company = low pay. When I commented on the rent being high, he responded by saying, “maybe it is a lot for you.”

I told him it is a terrible financial decision.
I also told him that I live in a major city and my monthly taxes alone are more than that.

Later, I spoke with another guy who pays around $2,000 a month for a luxury apartment. That’s about $24,000 a year. I didn’t ask about his income or job situation, but I shared that I thought the annual rent cost seemed very high.

I kept telling him his rent was a waste of money and he should buy a house. He told me he might not want to to live somewhere for so long. I asked him how he expected to build wealth. The conversation went back and forth with me saying it was a waste of “money down the drain.” He eventually admitted it was hard and bad use of money and insulted me with “life is hard when YOU don’t live in mommies basement.”
Wow.
He is literally throwing money down the drain and I trying to give me good advice.

It made me wonder if people commonly spend over $20,000–$30,000 a year on rent? And if so, how common is that relative to income and lifestyle choices?

Please look at picrel. Do people spend so much of their paycheque on rent? How does anyone become rich?

Curious what others think about this.

Early replies (first 3)

62372611
>>62372604
>I talked to two people today about rent.
no you didn't

62372621
>$24k per year rent is throwing money down the drain
>$50k per year interest payments for 30 years to "own" the same thing is building wealth

Lol

Lmao

62372624
>>62372604

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62377165
>>62372604
This chart is bogus. I make $80,000 a year and bought a house for $300,000 back in 2024. My monthly take home is no where near $10,000

62377214
>>62377090
Did you read the op…?

62377226
>>62376833
> is not high in most places these days. If you equate that to a house payment that is under $300k which in major metros is ghetto tier housing.
Kek>>62377165
>>62376833
> If you want to invest and get rich, stay under 25% of your take-home.
You’re not rich if you don’t own a house and everything is just tied up the market. lol.
That’s why the home ownership class in good areas are so far ahead.
Wealth gap, once again.

62377272
i own a few duplexes

every year i raise the rent and every year the tenties explain why they cant afford it

then they renew the lease anyway

its honestly kinda inspiring

62377298
Another thread where 80 IQ mongoloids with a 3% mortgage try doing the math that has already been done a million times showing that renting + equities > home ownership from a financial point of view

Btw if you stop paying property taxes the government takes your house

62377350
>>62377298
rent's due on the 1st

increasing $50

thank you for your service

62377435
>>62377350

Oh no, the landlord raised rent for the 6 month lease by 40 minutes of work a month ):

Meanwhile:

62377512
>>62372937
Huh? Who leases for more than a year? My apt has up to 15 months so I do that. But it goes up 4% every time.

I do put about $10k/yr into the market but my rent is like 50 fucking % of my after tax pay. I reconnected with my long lost dad so I have a parent with a house finally, but not sure if I'd move in just to save $25k/yr when I'll be 34 and long past should have started a family by then

62377522
>>62377435
you can show this to the leasing office when you renew

62377531
>>62372604
im sorry but every home is 300k+ and not everyone is making 10k a month.

62377592
>>62377298
invalid comparison

there's no reason you can't own a home and put just as much money into stocks.

rent isn't free. It costs as much or more than a mortgage. That's money you're throwing away

62377725
>>62372604
>mortgage is 14% of my monthly NET income.
Neighborhood is upper middle class white people and Asians.
Feels good

62377937
>>62377725
Imagine crawling into a moist bed every day. Pool rooms put off so much moisture that the humidity would make your sheets sticky every day. Not only that, but God forbid you splash and your bed is now straight up soaked. Oh yeah a and who wants to smell like pool chemicals every day? This is so retarded, but it would be fun for 45 minutes.

62377944
urbanite trash vs rural chads

62378180
>>62377937
I believe that was just a staged photo anon, chill.

62378190
Hmmmm
>rent cheap with enough spare income to invest in the stock market
Or
>pay an expensive mortgage with Insurance just to be house broke and own the property in 30 years

Ownership is good for the sake of owning but it is such a shitty investment vehicle.

62378268
>>62372604
Look man tell me I'm wrong or that I'm coping or that I'm stupid I don't care what you say to me but this shit genuinely sounds like it didn't happen. I don't know why, but whenever you make these threads about you speaking to people or the gym people going home to count their money it just comes off as a made up scenario that only happened inside your head. I don't know what you're trying to do or if you need meds or maybe I need meds but holy shit it just reads like cringe imaginary scenarios. Maybe you're just autistic and need some form of outlet or something...

62378368
>>62372604
>Small company = low pay
What?

62378369
>>62378180
IDK, there was a lot of crazy shit in the 70s.

>>62378268
Definitely never happened, can't care what OP says because they deny it every time

62378396
>get mortgage
>miss a payment by a picosecond on your last month
>lose the house and 20 years of your life
Maybe if we lived in an era where you could keep the same job forever, i would not be afraid, but nowadays you are gambling on the grueling torture of the job market after an unavoidable layoff

62378653
>>62372937
LOL delusional. People with mortgages before 2021 are paying less than renters with lower loan amounts and 2% interest rate, a modern aristocracy if you will. Renters live at the mercy of their landlords

62380330
>>62378653
Life is complicated, so some people forget the fundamentals of /biz/: Buy low, sell high.

Historically, mortgage rates are in "average" territory but the Price-to-Rent ratio is still near its all time high.

Why buy the top?

62380440
I would rather spend 30k a year on rent and live in a white/Asian area than 'own' a home I can afford that's in a latinx/black area. I don't have 100k liquid for a down payment on a white neighborhood home sadly.

62381017
>>62372621
You can still take the money and leave your property taxed shithole.

62381312
>>62372604
Where do you live that you make 120,000 after tax per year that has a 300,000 house?

62381768
>>62377226
Money tied up in a home performs far worse than money tied up in the market, historically speaking. People are just too dumb to leave the money alone unless it is tied up in home equity. Stock market investing is too liquid for the average person with no self control .
>I can just click here and here and then spend it all on tattoos and rims!

62383051
>>62372604
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62383177
>>62377298
it all depends on how much you invested initially

it was $113 to move into my house that i bought for 325k in 2018... that house is now worth 550k

that's a 2000x on initial investment, stocks aren't doing that unless you nail something like sandisk

62383209
>>62374484
Yeah I recognize his posting style, he was the guy bragging about his nonexistent rich friend to zoomers on Fortnite. He is extremely mentally ill.

62383213
Plus nobody rents in Atlanta for 2400 dollars, and certainly wouldn’t live in downtown Atlanta where it’s nog central. Even Buckhead is cheaper than that and everyone just commutes there anyway

item.title: > age 34 > drained down to last $20k — 50 replies — 58.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
> age 34 > drained down to last $20k

Open thread • Replies: 50 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:24 UTC

OP

> literally impossible to get just a fucking interview these days
> once had $160k nw (circa 2022) but combination of awful timing decisions with crypto + 4 years unemployed did a toll on me with living expenses
> entrepreneurship is hypersaturated beyond belief, impossible to make money online now
> AI is slaughtering every money making field out there so I can't figure out which masters degree or cert I need to go for next and they're all so fucking expensive too

I'm so scared bros HELP

Early replies (first 3)

62382538
>unemployed 4 years
lmao, imagine being this worthless

62382547
>>62382527
just think if you had bought chip stocks instead of fucking around with crypto you would probably be a millionaire.

62382548
>>62382538
fuck off nigger ur probably some 19 year old shithead u dont know anything life will rape u and humble u faggot
>>62382527
Dont worry about it anon im sure u can find a decent job soon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382625
>>62382580
cool you could have also put the down payment in nvidia and been retired

62382627
>>62382527
might depend on your area but water treatment plant operators are in demand all over the US
you'll have to work for a shit wage until you get the hours for your license. you mentioned masters degree and this would be easy in comparison, not to mention no debt since most places pay / reimburse
easy 30-40/h after a year spent getting the req hours
I don't live in a major city so I find the pay good, easy to get into because most people smart enough to do it do something else

62382631
>>62382527
>>62382548
Nigga, four years? At a certain point you're just a bum.

62382648
>>62382631
Shit ass job market, 2022 was when the tech bubble started to pop then 2023 AI reared it's ugly head and made everything 100x worse. Fast forward to today and the job market keeps getting worse and worse by the day. For the record I'm not looking for just remote jobs either, and every job has 100+ applicants within the first half hour of posting.

62382653
>>62382648
>Shit ass job market
You had an entire Bachelor's degree of time to figure something out.

62382655
>>62382527
Buy link you dumb faggot

62382660
>>62382625
That wouldn't be enough to retire.

62382662
>>62382631
kek shut up wagie goy cattle

62382669
>>62382655
Buy an ad stinkie

62382670
>>62382662
>goy cattle
You think the Jews are sitting around all day NEETing playing bing bang wahoos?
No nigga they're charging Cletus 30% interest for a 72 month loan on his King Ranch.

62382672
>>62382561
Should've lifted with your knees

62382673
>>62382670
you wouldn't know, best to stay in your lane

62382675
>>62382673
>you wouldn't know
I know more than you.

62382678
>>62382672
>knees don't blow out
>it is scolio
no lifting form could have ever prevented this.

62382681
If not a shill demoralizing thread, get CDL and get a local hourly LTL job. It’s easy money once you learn how to use the truck safely. Also includes just the right amount of physicality so you won’t be a fat ass

62382683
>>62382675
>t. retard.

62382688
>>62382683
I'm right.

62382695
>>62382688
>t.

62382735
>>62382527
if you think you are fucked with 20k left i really hope you run down to 0 so you see how good you actually have it now

62382754
WHO CARES NIGGUH IRAN WON WOOOO

62382755
>>62382548
nah you could have gotten a job in 4 years you're just stupid and will remain poor for life.

62382778
>>62382670
Yeah. Jews literally just Neet and collect welfare. That is unironically what Hasidic jews do.

62382792
>>62382527
>33 years old
>6'3"
>190 pounds
>Agent 47 Chad facial features
>6 pack abs
>$720k net worth
>Live in the middle of nowhere
>Ohio
>Virgin

62382803
>>62382612
Whatever gaylord, entrepreneurship isn’t for cowards like you

>>62382648
Is this OP? If so, >>62382653 is right.
You also had 160k to figure something out.
What are your skills? If you’re tech savvy at all, why not use AI for something to sell like an app or two? Why not make AI porn profiles on social media?
What networking events did you attend?

62383064
>>62382527
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62383084
>>62382538
>>62382631
You fuckers want a job? Isn't the whole point of this board getting rich enough to NEET eternally

62383305
>>62382648
>then 2023 AI reared it's ugly
Nigga you dum as hell. No one was laid off in 2023 because of AI. Probably the numbers in 2026 are still 4 digits globally.

62383334
>>62382648
learn chinese nigga. i work for chinese companies. in tech, the salary is amazing and you get white privelege. sucks to not have a niche. i got another offer for even more money yesterday just through guanxi

62383336
>>62382792
Kek

62383357
>>62383336
It's true, lol

item.title: >UK less than someone in Mississippi >UK is poorer than all of 50 American states >Gets taxed 41% if you make anywhere n… — 27 replies — 30.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>UK less than someone in Mississippi >UK is poorer than all of 50 American states >Gets taxed 41% if you make anywhere n…

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:23 UTC

OP

>UK less than someone in Mississippi
>UK is poorer than all of 50 American states
>Gets taxed 41% if you make anywhere north of 40k
>Walk outside of London and it gets much worse
....

How do Europeans even live? Is all of the UK just a backwater rural wasteland outside of London?

Early replies (first 3)

62377614
>>62377608
yes lawyers make 20k in the UK lmao

62377646
>>62377608
Who cares

62377666
>>62377646
Im sure the poor sons of bitches in the UK care bro even their extremely educated make dog shit piss ass money

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62377670
>>62377646
Anglicans are beneath even those from backwater rural states in America.

62377673
>>62377646
Well the mass exodus of professional class

62378733
>>62377608
The London experiment, to be a tax haven for modern wealth and create the NYC of Europe, has failed. They all fled to Dubai.

62378738
You have to remember that England still has a countryside. While it's the worst victim of the NGO woke Soros political shenanigans you could still break up some functioning parts in the future as London inevitably collapses

62378777
>>62377608
In the UK people are plumbers and electricians

62378898
>>62377608
>Yeah bro, we're totally living in squalid third world conditions. Life is hellish and brutal on our quiet walks through sleepy 99% white villages with centuries-old buildings. That's why British people are falling over each other to emigrate to the paradise that is Mississippi. Moron.
If you had a passport you could go to Europe yourself. American quality of life is terrible because you either live in a niggerfied urban crime dystopia or you live in a zoned gated community full of boomers with no amenities. When we look at places to emigrate we look at places like Switzerland, not fucking muttland...

62378901
>>62378898
complete bollocks. many people going to the US for work all the time and even more aspiring to.

62378927
>>62378901
Net migration is from the USA to UK dude... If you're actually successful in the UK you get a second home in some rich part of Europe like Switzerland as I mentioned, or my business partner has property in the Dolomites. America hasn't been an aspirational destination for decades, same with Australia and Canada.

62379008
I bring in £2k a month take home pay, which buys me all of nothing for a months work.
Grim and once I get my inheritance I am out of here.

62379022
>>62377608
>GDP
UK is a shithole but GDP is a retarded nonsense metric. all economists are on a one way trip to nigger hell.

62379027
>>62379022
>muh lights on choinese skycrappers and shit

62379038
>>62379027
if lights and clean streets are so cheap then why can't the so-called richest country in the world pull it off?

62379040
>>62379027
>>62379038
also the real dividing characteristic in the picture is a single high rise building vs multiple high rise buildings, a massive bridge, public transportation, etc.

62383050
>>62378898
England is abysmal, don’t be retarded. It’s only good if you are upper class, there are next to no jobs and they don’t hire white people. Everything has a retarded license attached to it, like AC, flooring, even shit like getting a drivers license costs thousands of dollars. Even public transport can take hours and is unreliable
>t. gf is British

62383053
>>62377608
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62383093
>>62377608
They're called Europoors for a reason. The sad part is they actually don't comprehend how poor they actually are. There's a bunch of post on socials of Europeans coming to America for the world cup and being blown away by everything.

62383097
>>62377608
The figure in the UK is horseshit too btw. They never include "part time" workers and often only report household figures, as though it's 1960.
Incomes in the UK are pitiful and massively uneven. If you are outside of London (and don't work on a nuclear power plant in Cumbria) then you're doing well if you make 40k. This hasn't really changed for almost two decades because our economy is so crystalline and stagnant.
Increasingly, we are turning into Russia where the capital is an economic black hole. Most of the provinces aren't simply neglected they are actively deprived because boomers erupt in rage as the prospect of distrubing their "cute" countryside retirement home. We are basically cold Florida with DC and its suburbs attached.
PPP is complete nonsense as well. Shit is expensive here it isn't like Spain or Greece where things are actually cheap. A lot of stuff is even cheaper in the US.

62383101
>>62377608
US just has more billionaires. Stop this cope. You do not deserve to feel pride in your shithole country.

62383110
>>62383101
Lmao I don't believe for one millisecond that thr average Dutch person has less wealth than the average Briton and certainly the average French person. Dutch people are more likely to own a home and housing there is about 60% more expensive. Not to mention incomes are better.
We are just very good at cherrypicking and cheating data here. It's an ordeal trying to find transparent figures for almost anything.

62383293
>>62379022

Back in 2020 in a rare chinese government report released showed that almost 1 billion chinese people earn less than $280/month, 600 million under $140/month.

62383295
>>62383293

Forgot to post pic

62383317
>>62383295
> <=0
>5,460,000 people
i wonder how many make less than 0

62383344
>>62377608
And Japan makes even less.
Yet anyone with a working brain would pick Japan over Missisipiss. Wonder why.

62383354
>>62377608
That's what happens when your empire collapses (america is next btw)

item.title: SPCX tomorrow — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SPCX tomorrow

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-18 09:03 UTC

OP

SPCX tomorrow

Early replies (first 3)

62383211
>>62383168
The hype is already over. It's collapsing

62383214
God I hope so
I want to buy more cheapies when I grt paid

62383240
feel bad for my parents for aping in. i told them to wait 6 months, alas.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383284
I aped my entire $600k IRA and brokerage portfolio into SPCX. If this goes belly-up, I will quite literally kms!

62383407
so is there any reason to not short it right now?

62383420
Five minutes

item.title: >he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family? — 11 replies — 19.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:42 UTC

OP

>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters

Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Early replies (first 3)

62379163
you're asking why I didn't invest in my family...

and I will ask in return...

Is killing niggers free and open-source?

62379166
I personally view having kids an essential life goal.
Just give me more time.

62379173
>>62379160
I'm 29 with $300k I'm gonna get my gf pregnant at 30. Hopefully I'll have 400k by the

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379191
>>62379160
I'm trying, bro.

62379204
>>62379166
>I just need to complete this massive list of things that likely won't happen before I start a family

the equivalent of waiting for a big price dip in a good stock that you subconsciously know isn't going to happen

62379219
>>62379160
Bad ROI and infinite P/E.

62379223
>>62379204
Having kids is like being a cuck in a relationship. You watch from the sidelines as others fullfill their lives while you wait your sloppy seconds.

62379248
>>62379223
Lol

62379259
Next bullrun when I'm 40-something I'll find a prime 25 year old girl to marry and have children with.

It's not too late r-right?

62383378
>>62379259
No, but you'll have to accept that she only likes you for money

62383390
>>62379259
You are an old hag, it is so creepy

item.title: I am most ashamed that I was too stupid to see how this Chainlink shit would end; or that I didn’t wait till I better un… — 11 replies — 19.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am most ashamed that I was too stupid to see how this Chainlink shit would end; or that I didn’t wait till I better un…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:41 UTC

OP

I am most ashamed that I was too stupid to see how this Chainlink shit would end; or that I didn’t wait till I better understood the situation here in the higher levels of the chains leadership before I sold $LINK as a dream to my friends, my family, and even mine and my friends companies.

Personally did harm to my friends by selling them this shit.

Definitely learned to never trust narratives, dig deeper than face value and better test the tech before promoting it; for anything moving forward.

I was absolutely in the cult of Sergey; only difference between me and most is I was blocked by Chainlink Labs for disagreeing with narratives before I knew the full picture (circa 2026)

Early replies (first 3)

62377672
>>62377660
Your friends are dumb as hell for trusting a retard. Chainlink your balls to my face.

62377692
>>62377660
At least you learned something out of all this OP, also never push investment choices on anyone else just in case it does blow up and you look like a retard. People will always brush off and forget proportional to medium size wins but will never forget a loss. It's happened to me once, never again.

62380598
>>62377660
Cucklink = Russian ponzi scheme

Sergey is the only person who /madeit/ with Chainlink

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62381301
>>62377660
idk anon we haven't even really started the game yet and you're calling the outcome? LINK lives or dies by its tradfi adoption. 2026 will be the first year we see some of that come online and it'll be years before we see the real adoption curve play out.

62381444
>>62381301
>Just 2 more weeks bro

Is what a fuddie would like to post, unwilling to provide a real retort to this argument

62381930
>>62381444
Trips, Kek really wants me to sell (nice try Kek)

62381939
>>62381301
>2018 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2019 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2020 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2021 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2022 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2023 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2024 will be the first year we see some of that come online
>2025 will be the first year we see some of that come online

62382102
>>62381939
>2026 chainlink turned me gay

62382292
please don't sell, ok? please

62382347
these disingenuous larps and appeals are very gross
if any of it is true then you have no one to blame but yourself, retard, and whining on a mongolian sheep breeding bulletin board while addressing nothing of substance beyond ridiculous claims of causing harm by proffering financial advice is peak faggotry
but who am I kidding, it's the same band of mentally ill freak faggots it always is

62383388
>>62382347
>disingenuous
you overuse this word to the point it's clear you're projecting

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 57 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-18 05:47 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62374835
>4 figure club thread
>half the posts are animeposters
Many such cases

62374867
>>62373798
Mah nigga this is just Gabriel-chan. She's a neet that didn't do shit about her angelic job, her demon friends started helping her out. Yes, she will be a perfect base for our mascot.

62374886
>>62373888
Based trips.

>Next order of business.
>$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$

The way i see it, you guys need to learn how to DCA. That is easy, just buy whatever you want on monthly basis. The thing that we need to understand is: which ones to buy? Well I find that /smg/ threads are great for that.

>But we're poorfags who cant even invest 100 bucks on something
Yea, me too son, me too

62375712
>>62373752
how to make money as a poorfag /biz/raeli guide:
>prolific ai shit. bery klaustrophian
>work a wage job
>lie cheat and steal; have no remorse about it
>a penny saved is a penny earned. literally.
>unlearn your poorfag tendencies and your scarcity mindset trauma
>steal from homeless people
>street busking on off days
put everything on the stock market. fuck crypto.
>don't cheap out on food though. good nutrition makes for better decisions
>if ur on meds, start selling them
>dont be an autist who views the law as an unbreakable fact of life.
>get into options trading if you can. read the options playbook
>stfu about your money adventures
>SHUT THE FUCK UP ABOUT MONEY
>DO NOT TELL ANY1 IRL ABOUT WHAT YOU'RE DOING

62375724
>>62374533
QUBT,RGTI,IONQ

i had cheap entry prices but sold too early. now i want to kill myself

62376678
Bump, will be off work soon

62376699
>>62373803
Not sure if you noticed but you are engaging with bots in a bot thread

62376812
>>62376678
Still wagecucking, we left off at figuring out how to profit from making art. Want me to create a thread on /ic/ to invite drawfags?

62376829
>>62373772
Which eggregore is most predatory to us poor fags? Have our new eggregore waifu kill them

62376867
>>62376829
That's easy, it's called the grabbler.
https://youtu.be/Q4PC8Luqiws?si=wQBcUv8QOcxDJAXE

62377559
>>62376812
Drawfag days are numbered
They are gay, over-socialized and love the status quo
You would probably have better results recruiting AI "slop" artists from /g/
Despite what the sour grapes gatekeeping "artists" say, AI people put a lot of thought effort in their craft

62377563
>>62374193
BBWI

62377801
Okay guys


Here's what I have:

1. We scour the archive for leaks, gems and potential moon shots. It will be akin to searching through the dump for retro consoles. I looked through the archives and saw like 10 people invested into Palantir before it was big so I'm not too hopeful on /biz/ being the place for breadcrumbs but you never know. LINK was shilled early here.

2. We work together to look at certain patterns through events in the last 20 years. Let's not pretend reality isn't scripted. For instance! If you believed that SPACEX would rise BECAUSE Elon already had some gay scripted event with the SEC and TSLA back in '17 or '18. You would've made good money. No need for fundamentals. Just schizo and occult vibes.

3. We work on building better resumes. Lie. Lie. Lie. We find the best WFH jobs, or jobs that would NEVER hire us and "modify" our resumes to look better. Personally I hate waging and this would remind me of high school wage preparatory classes. Also this is kind of Jeety and interferes with my Weltanschauung.

4. The Drawfags idea as per
>>62373902

He will have to tell us what this Idea is and NO we are not leaving /biz/


5. The TCG pokemon way, as per >>62374140 Not a bad idea seeing as there is already a TCG general. We will have to be willing to dirty our hands with pokeniggers. If it ends poorly then maybe we can impress 18-19 art hoes with our cards.

6. We can also learn Photonics as per >>62374533 Rigorous study, I would purchase the udemy course and we all hit the books and discuss daily who has the best light tech. Probably will be boring.

Gentleman, please contribute. We need all our finest minds

GET THE FUCK OUT IF YOU HAVE MORE THAN $10,000

>>62374471
>>62374086
>>62373881

62377818
>>62377801
Also we want to pick something and stick with it. At least for 30 days to 3 months. If it isn't working then it isn't working. I don't see why ALL of us can't make it to $10k. It would also be best if we pick a route that if it doesn't work out then we at least learned something. When you invest in a shit coin and it 200x and goes to $0, what did you learn compared to someone who started an indoor shrimp farming who had a good run but had to declare bankruptcy? What REAL WORLD skills were obtained? Nothing?

62377841
>>62374042

62377846
We'll all be rich!

62377858
/biz/ is slow these days, I will check back in an hour. More suggestions would be lovely. Then we unanimously agree on something or maybe just roll for it in true 4chan fashion.

62377867
>>62377801
>He will have to tell us what this Idea is and NO we are not leaving /biz/
I am going to slowly invite drawfags from /ic/ to get at least one of them to collaborate with us and figure out how to sell art whether it be through patreon, crypto, whatever means to make a nickel. I only know of two, one of them is just beginning to draw again and doodles the cutest bears.
>>62377858
I'd like to believe a lot of us wagecuck and simply do not have the time of day but we'll make something happen.

62377880
7. We scour /smg/ as per >>62374886 and report back to this thread with the underrated, most talked about and genuinely good picks. Since there are multiple /smg/ threads this would be great.

NOTE: We should just use the current /smg/ threads and the archived ones at least within the past 1-2 years. So if you know a stock that everyone is talking about on reddit, please die. Reddit is not /smg/. Our pool that we search through will be /smg/. It would be best as it would help narrow our search and some people on /smg/ really do know their stuff. There is an individual there bragging daily about how he told everyone to buy Intel.

I am open to criticism and suggestions

62377886
>>62373752
If you actually want to get out of five figures your best bet is a six figure salary. I'm not joking, invest in yourselves.

62377896
if you have earned income and don’t have a retirement account, I would open a Roth IRA in addition to a regular taxable investment account. get in the habit of making regular contributions to both accounts. automatic deposits would be best if possible. even if you don’t make a lot, getting in the habit of investing is worthwhile. it’s best to invest in an etf like VTI or an s&p 500 etf. if there are dividends, set up automatic dividend reinvestment. (If you have a taxable account you’ll still need to pay tax on dividends, even if you reinvest them).

62378002
>>62373918
Well the /ic/ thread got deleted within minutes this is why I am cautious whenever we start to plan or congregate together its like they hate that.

62378439
>>62374533
Photonics sounds solid
I base all of the following off my schizo intuition:

1. I keep hearing about how Elon musk is a retard and SpaceX IPO is a rug pull. But you don't get that rich by being stupid.

2. I can't see X ever being profitable (though it is valuable for other reasons), and nobody gives a shit about electric cars anymore. But SpaceX... he is throwing money on that for a reason.

3. And Starlink is the world's biggest photonics deployment in space right now.
Satellite internet + photonics is going to be a huge deal
- cheap, super fast internet (terabits per second) that doesnt depend on today's bloated over-regulated infrastructure, and you can access it from anywhere
- Governments - specially EUcucks - keep making it difficult for you to use the internet and have free speech, could get BTFO the same way as the twitter trannies did when elon bought X
- Any retard shithole country with underwater drones can cut underwater internet cables, shooting down a satelite would be more difficult
- Perfect opportunity for a psyop to push people into using starlink instead of traditional intenet

If you couple that with AI companies also wanting photonics for their datacenters, you could get a lot of money this way

62378965
Brothers,
Just learn how to use the order book in polymarket, it's full of idiots that are willing to buy your bags for absurd prices. We can set our profile pictures to anime pic rel >62373866 to coordinate, target events like the current fifa event and mass shill or fud a team or group of teams so we can pump our bags
Two days ago i made 4K by just selling Belgium to win the world cup at 3x the actual price and some nigger actually bought it

62379061
>>62377886
Vague platitude

Option number 8 by >>62378965

There could be gains to be made on polymarket. Many of us are American so we'd either use kalshi or a vpn

The anon was so kind to give us an excerpt on his success and /biz/ has yet to make an impact on these markets like it did in 2021 in the BSC casino

By the end of the day, we shall have a decision set

62380691
Bump

62381251
Bump

62381343
>>62378002
/ic/ is filled with schizoids and endless seethe. And this is coming from someone who draws as a hobby.

62381797
>>62373752
>4 figure hell
unironically get a job, any job
like just work a year lol

62383107
It's settled, tune in tomorrow!

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 24 replies — 15.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-06-18 07:43 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383127
LINK will never be above $20 again. You missed your chance.

62383130
Ok, I'm just not going to sell tho.

62383132
>>62383127
$20 is a 2.5x from here
Not only that, that's barely 20 billion extra in MC
The upside potential of link is monstrous, swingies get the fucking rope

62383155
>>62383132
Bro cmon. I should had sold everything at 40, even 30 and forget about link forever.

62383192
>>62382813
Do one for spacex for us now please
Ahahahahahhaha

62383251
>>62382842
>>62382990
This

62383254
>>62382813
Is it a good time to buy yet or not? We have been trying to decide for years

62383255
>>62383132
Overused buzzwords, here a few more
>the price is bad because they are supressing it not because i picked an underperforming asset
>lol it's a cult of course i ignore any rationale that goes against sergey
>staking is already live
>ok yeah the fees are going to cll instead of stakers but they're doing buy backs
>sure they dump 17 million tokens and rebuy 100k of them, but buybacks man
>yeah swift and dtcc are going live without the "necessary" decentralized oracle network, but they will totally need it later on for some reason

62383260
I want a linkmarine to ram my pussy

62383263
>>62383255
Post your holdings and your hand

62383265
I'm selling in 2028/2029. I still remember back in 2021 when LINK was $45 and I kept debating with myself whether to sell or not and decided not to. That shit is going to haunt me for the rest of my life.

62383267
>>62383255
>>the price is bad because they are supressing it
yes, see pic

>>sure they dump 17 million tokens and rebuy 100k of them
We've known about the supply unlocks for 9 years, we've known about the buybacks for less than a year

>swift and dtcc are going live without the "necessary" decentralized oracle network
Every single Chainlink product uses the decentralized oracle network. What a bizarre thing to just lie about.

62383269
>>62383263
>no i totally don't hold the token, i just know everything about it to more accuratelly fud
*cries in third world*

62383274
>>62383267
>the switch bro's, the switch is flipped
>surely the price must be pushing higher from all these onchain buy fees towards the link token
>wait
>why is everything a cash deal between that company and cll
>why is cll paying their own nodes

62383275
>>62383274
>>why is cll paying their own nodes
Because cll runs the contract.

Holy shit you're retarded

62383279
>>62383275
You see this lurkers? Another layer of cope comes forth.

62383287
>>62383279
I love how you always pretend you have this huge silent audience. This board is dead, maybe 15 people will ever see your posts, and 14 of them will instantly know you're a clueless retard.

62383291
>>62383287
I love how you always pretend you have this answer for brownian motion. This board is an echo chamber, maybe 15 people will ever see your posts, and 14 of them will instantly know you're the same as them.

62383298
>>62383291
>same as them
>same skin color as them
Sorry, I want to add that to the end of my post. Makes it way funnier.

62383299
Ram my pussy bitches

62383312
>>62383299
Sorry femanon, the marines have been beating their dicks senseless to anime jpegs for years. I don't think they can keep it up for a real woman. They'll probably cum too fast and watch some bull finish you.

item.title: BREAKING NEWS: INSIDERS SPECULATE AND CONFIRMED: MATT DAMON COMING TO RIPPLE — 16 replies — 10.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BREAKING NEWS: INSIDERS SPECULATE AND CONFIRMED: MATT DAMON COMING TO RIPPLE

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-18 07:42 UTC

OP

This is some **MASSIVE** amazing news for the XRP and crypto community as a whole. None other than Matt Damon is coming to Ripple Swell!!!

You Are Not Bullish Enough

Early replies (first 3)

62383008
>>62382926
>Matt Damon, co-founder of water
HE FOUNDED WATER
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62383013
WATER RUNS ON XRP

62383017
DIS NIGGA INVENTED WATER

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383027
>>62383008
>>62383013
>>62383017
You retards

>Water
>Liquid
>Liquidity
>Drops
>Ripple
>Swell

62383034
The CEO of bullish is coming Ripple Swell too....
>>62383027
it's too bullish

62383116
Oh look, crippletardie paypigs are getting all riled up for another nothingburger S(W)ELL conference once again. Surely, this year will be different. Or maybe the next, or the one after that.

62383163
>>62382926
Aaaahhh so this is how they are creating cripple fags, they are drinking this tap water and fucking up their brains kek.

62383166
Fortune favors the bold
>nukes market

62383173
Remember those crypto.com ads
>Not bullish

62383221
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62383239
Boom

62383242
Matt Damon is gonna science the shit out of it!!!!

62383243
IT WAS SO FUNNY WATCHING RIPPLE FUCKING SEETHE BY HAVING THEIR CONFERENCE THE SAME TOWN AMD DATE AS CHAINLINKS SMARTCON AND LITERALLY EVERYONE IMPORTANT WENT TO SMARTCON INSTEAD HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

62383246
>>62383243
>smartcon
>smart
>con

anon.....

Never follow the crowd, the crowd is massively retarded

Ripple is the chosen one not chainlink

62383268
>>62383246
Sar

62383311
>>62383243
Ripple also held Swell at the same time as Swift's Sibos in 2017.

They're so fucking goofy.

item.title: How is your career going? — 44 replies — 50.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:23 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

Early replies (first 3)

62381055
Going for the NICET Fire Alarm Systems Level 1 license in the next 2 months or so. Will hopefully get to $30/hr.

62381060
>>62381049
*chirp*

62381078
>>62381049
is that a fun job? sounds comfy desu

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382290
>>62382261
Hmmm you made me think more about it. The one that went off was on the second floor, but directly below it was the main entrance. I wonder if that one is controlled by the alarm so people can use the main entrance as an exit if there is a fire.

62382303
>>62381049
Weekly hours? Opportunities for part time at that rate?

62382307
uhh i make $23 an hour but its salaried with decent vacation and low social interaction

62382353
>>62381049
terrible im unemployed for 7 months and cant get interviews at best buy

62382431
>>62382303
Anywhere from 35-40 hours with overtime coming regularly during some stretches of the year. As far as I know there is no part-time role.

62382434
>>62382290
Sadly, as cool as that would be,you can't control which sprinkler goes off unless you light a lighter under it, or break it with a hammer haha

62382436
>>62382184
Pretty comfy, but its closer to learning an instrument and you might have to sit in court all day depending on what you are doing. Not a court reporter though. Also the equipment is kind of pricey. But, you can get deals on student gear.

62382484
>>62381049
In like 1973 you’d be based. You are a fag now.

62382503
>>62382484
Why 1973?

62382522
>>62381049
I make over 90k as an IT Manager for a multi-state company with around 180 employees. No certs, no degree. They were a companies client I worked for and they were impressed with my work. You and I are not the same.

62382530
>>62382522
How did you get started?

62382539
>>62381049
career?

62382549
180k operations manager. Adult babysitter. Plan to be retired in 5 years if investments keep going the right way, sick of this shit.

62382558
I make ~$30-50/hr CAD delivering pizza depending on the shift, but I top out at 27 hours a week.

62382757
I inspect different warehouses for inventory of different stock. Basically just write numbers on a count sheet and then submit it. Get paid 100k/yr plus I got 160k in stocks in tax free accounts.

I guess it beats micromanaged cubicle white collared work and back breaking blue collared labor. Just gonna do this until I can retire in my mid 40s

62382779
Not great
> I used to be a great coder with bad soft skills
> AI has made soft skills more important than coding
> I hate sitting in front of a computer all day but it pays at least 3X what I could get in a more physical job

62382786
>>62381049
i'm a software engineer, fucking hate it because i got promoted beyond the point of writing code. i actually love writing code and i just want to build shit, but now that's no longer my job and it's pretty fucking gay all around

62382789
>>62382786
Writing code is extremely boring with Claude anyway, and refusing to use it is not an option

62382806
35 an hour doing monkey data entry (accounts payable) at a hospital. I have a bachelors in accounting and this is my first entry level job. Before this I worked at an Amazon warehouse. Took me a whole 5 years to get an office job. I barely tried though so it’s my fault
t. 28

62382818
>>62381049
I've been stuck in public service for 12 years doing work I hate. I recommended my coworker who turned out to be a complete fucking idiot once he started working here. Hes going to get us both fired. I have missed out on better opportunities here because I am too nice and didn't advocate for myself. Its all so tiresome.

62382822
>>62381049
Have you identified which government regulations secure your job?
Seems like an important thing to know.

62382877
>>62381049
27m, 150k, fully remote. on track to become senior and make ~180k

swe

62382899
>>62381049
24m
$45/hr as a Information Management/Technical Consultant.
Been at it for 4 years:
-Mostly just showing people how to organize data and operate basic proprietary corpo programs.
-90% of the work is physical (walking in, seeing something has obviously been done wrong, fixing it)
Job security is that people will always have trouble with the machines they use on a day to day basis. People will always be stupid and mess things up.

>>62382184
On the equipment side, stenotype's have been around since the 1910's so you can easily get comfortable with the layout on a typewriter from the 1950's for $30.

>>62382303
>Weekly hours?
It's rural, my usual is roughly 30 hours. I get calls from 100+ miles away who are willing to pay enough to have me come out and teach.
A lot of the extra dough comes from just claiming what the company wants to through out tech wise, then wiping and reselling their entire server (made in 2021) online or in person through third party marketplaces. Make about double what the hourly is with that.

>>62382530
>how did you get started
I joke to a lot of the upper class clients "no one hired me, so I hired myself"

>>62382877
Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

62382947
>>62382899
>Is it niche work? How do you prevent your job from getting jeeted out?

we have some foreign contractors but we have a shit ton of funding covering burn so we can afford to hire citizens. it's not a unique business but we're niche in our approach and field (data/ai). we're actually hiring right now.

62383011
33m 110k a year working in crypto, remote.
Workplace changed from based and nice to kinda idiotic thanks to poor business decisions, AI brain rot, and limited runway.

I'm trying to change but everything else pays like shit or is a cult. IT makes me want to kill myself at this point. I have golden handcuffs

I just want to find a way to accumulate so much capital that I can live off dividends.

62383016
32k CAD ($20.25/hr) after being at WMT for 10 years
I know it's not the best job, but it's low effort, my coworkers and bosses and customers love me, and I can keep working sustainably. I've managed to get about 140k CAD net worth, too.

62383085
>>62382530
Basically I got hired from a craigslist posting for a middleman tech company ran by some bulgarian. He would send us out to clients and pay us less than half, scalping the rest. I have a legitimate interest in tech and build things with hardware, networks and software so some of these places I helped set up and was friendly helpful to them. It also let me learn the missing parts of each field and then I was able to piece it all together. Since they were the clients, I would interact with the CEOs, Owners and top end leadership of each company. You vibe well with them and not be inappropriate (hitting on, trying to get numbers, be a creep) they will want you, even do power plays to get you. I quit the middleman cause he would send me to do shit that gets paid 170k salary (85/hr) and pay me 21/hr and scalp the other 64, while not helping me really. I was able to chill for a year, then i reached out to one of these clients and they picked me right up. Since they wanted me, I had the leverage to negotiate a higher salary, which they had no problem paying. They liked me so much they just have me run the whole IT department, I dont even really have oversight. I do have to know my shit, but AI makes it 1000x easier and if you arent using AI you arent gonna make it. Literally complete tasks that would take months to do in days. These guys can't even fathom that kind of efficiency so they think im busy all the time. AI is literally a cheat code, especially if you know your shit and use it to do that shit. I'm close to 6 figs starting, will easily hit 6 figs after solve some of their money loss headaches (which i have done for a major part).
>Getting interest from potential employers gives you huge leverage, esp if they know you are a game changer
The best part is I asked if I could start my own projects for learning, that they would benefit the company and the CEO is hyped for it. I am literally getting paid to self improve

62383096
>>62381049
I'm a warehouse grunt
$20 / hour
How do I even go up from here? Maybe a forklift license?

62383304
I make 31 an hour doing quality assurance for asphalt and I think about killing myself every day.

62383355
>>62381049
I make $29/hour driving the city bus.
I have a class B CDL.
While the pay is nothing special, the benefits are incredible, the hours are great, and the company overall is a dream to work for.
I plan on leeching every last cent I can out of this place before getting a fat retirement in 30 years.

item.title: There are levels to this — 38 replies — 42.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
There are levels to this

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:22 UTC

OP

https://x.com/i/status/2067036752762847631

>intelligent, hard working, cosmopolitan women
>making over $300k in sales in New York

>typical 4channer
>limp dick racist making $45k in HVAC maintenance in Pawtucket, Missouri

Yeah, college is a scam, or something...

Early replies (first 3)

62381592
all so ugly why would any1 hire them

i know they are all jewish because why else would some1 hire such ugly women

62381660
>He isn’t racist

Why are you brown?

62381685
>>62381592
>>62381660
Stop bumping 0/10 threads, pls and thank you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62381863
>>62381543
Salespeople typically don't go to college anon.
Women get any jobs they want 300k starting even without any qualification by the way. It's 2026.

62381866
>>62381543
>Pawtucket, Missouri

62381882
>>62381543
who is hiring these women? they're not even eye candy tier but have ego's higher than low iq eye candy

62381889
>>62381592
Phenotypes check out
Obvious Chosen folx

62381890
>>62381882
Uggos and fatsos always have much higher egos than eye candy because their "peers" are handed jobs for free, so they feel that (by comparison) they have worked so unbelievably hard for the job they got (and all other things in life). They're also the ones that always go "muh patriarchy" unironically not realizing that 90% of men would kill to have even a fraction of the advantages they've received in life.

62381893
I bet 300 shekels I could do their jobs with a few days of training at most. But alas I'm a working class cracker so no dice

62381993
>>62381736
>pawtucket
you wanna meet up?

62382011
>>62381543
im just a humble neet chad. i don't get the appeal of the lifestyle these women have. all life they grind to look like fuckdolls and consume whatever their social media tells them to but beyond that its pretty empty in there

62382018
>>62381543
Fake adult day care job designed solely to preoccupy women from reproducing, employed by a company that produces very little if anything of actual value, kept afloat by a fake economy fueled by fake money and unpayable credit.

When this finally crashes it's going to crash hard

62382298
>>62381543
How does the average woman outside of NY fare?

62382582
>>62381543
i can't get a meme jew daycare job

62382600
>>62381543
>young

62382711
>>62381897
>Daily Mail
Nice try

62382722
>>62381543
Making 220-300k plus in Manhattan or the bay area sets your 401k up for life since you're matching 12% each year. If you buy a condo you can sell it when you're 29 and buy a million dollar home wherever you want

62382739
>>62381736
/thread

clean it up jannies

62382740
>>62381543
You sound like a gay retard, good luck with your 300k sales job thats totally real and is totally necessary. Should do well as the entire nation breaks down over the next few years

62382748
>>62382711
doesn't matter if its reuters or ap or new york times or daily mail, no such thing as trustworthy journalism. if you think otherwise you're naive. at least daily mail doesn't pretend to be anything it isn't, it knows its slop. the best most important stories no one else will cover next to the most trashy vapid nonsense. leftoid retards and right wing schizos alike publishing articles. daily mail is pure chaos and from that chaos sometimes the truths others won't tell.

62382830
>>62381863
Where’s my state sanctioned 300k job? I’d fucking love that

62382875
>>62381543
So you are telling me i can raise their rent even more? That's nice.

62382896
>>62381543
>mfw after I make 25 dollars after shoveling snow for 6 hours

62382969
>>62382896
You should check that rc car that she gave you, maybe there's a military chip inside.

62383040
>>62381543
These are the fuckers making other people life unaffordable moving from their shithole cities.

62383178
>>62381736
If you savemaxx with a 300k salary, its easy to save more than 45k,more than your wage.

62383184
>sales
yes, we already know the economy is worthless and roasties are overpaid
I bet there's a few doing HR which make almost as much in new york too, should ask them

62383278
Jerked off to this video.
The sheer injustice of some unremarkable bitch making over 300k a year for doing fuck all is just too hot.

62383281
>>62383184
Getting strong incel vibes from this post

62383351
Just say "yes" to everything. It's that easy.

item.title: What happened to META? — 17 replies — 24.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What happened to META?

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-18 08:33 UTC

OP

What happened to META?

Early replies (first 3)

62375138
>>62375027
What happened to Metaverse?

62375415
they completely whiffed on AI

62375425
2010s business model in 2020s

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378143
>>62375027
that crypto company? digial land isn't real nor scarce

oh wait you mean FACEBOOK that boomer company that old people use to post their goy opinions on?

LMAO

62378194
saaaaaaaaar

62378208
Despite their desperate clutching at straws they are struggling to stay relevant. Even MS which flopped absolutely everything they tried in the last 25 years is still on.

62378228
>>62375027
irrelevant company

62378234
>>62375027
It made too much profit. Top stocks can't make huge profits. There value needs to be hopium based.

62378483
>>62375027
literally nothing going for them anymore. metaverse failed. their AI models are garbage. their core products are tepid growth at best and more places are banning social media. only hope left is elon buying their data centers and gutting the rest.

62378502
>>62375138
>>62375415
/thread

Turns out you can't actually run a company by getting lucky with one idea (that was stolen)

62380977
>>62378483
their vr play is still king

62382772
>>62380977
I literally tried to play GOLF+ VR with my friends on my Meta Quest 2 earlier and it took nearly half an hour just to load. Meta OS recently had a shitty major release that is so bad that it disabled the in-game music feature. You used to be able to listen to youtube podcasts in GOLF+ and that doesn't even work anymore because Meta broke the integration

62382776
>>62382772
You ever gooned in it?

62382836
>>62375138
They goyim dealt in it

62382889
>>62375027
repeated failed projects like >>62375138
all their successes now are platforms they just bought like instagram
they do more than people realize but they can't be ranked next to apple and google as sure fire bets anymore imo

62383358
>>62375415
They are doing a lot of AI stuff especially in the open source sector.

62383373
>>62378143
retard, META owns Instagram, Facebook and Whatsapp. You're a retarded incel if you think they're niche normie platforms

62376208

62376220
>>62375128
Please post some reddit salt

62376256
>>62375128
Thank you for reminding me to buy some etf shorts lol

62376261
>>62376256
stupid nigger, SPCX is worth $14T. new target price is roughly $500 and that's conservative.

62376313
>>62376261
I'm shorting times 2 now. You happy yet?

62376334
>>62375128
Space X is Mooning to Mars!! Elon said he'd get us there

62376337
>>62375128
Are goys allowed to sell yet?

62376364
>>62375755
Satellites alone would be worth multiple trillions.

62376848

feels good man

62377060
>>62375393
Whatever nigger, he kept promising the Hyperloop, the robotaxis and Mars for next year since 2015.

>"But buh reddit"

Curious how Elon was the guy being praised by Onions individuals in reddit before he allied with Trump, now he is praised by le based onions individuals like you.

62377191
>>62375818
SpaceX isn’t even a space company. It’s an AI company (and underperforming shit AI) with a rocket hobby. You also only get access to 4% of the stock on retail. You’re begging for a giga rugpull.

62377238
>>62375128
AND A ZIONIST!!

62377697
>>62375128
hm, congrats to the buyers.

62379451
>>62375140
mathematically impossible valuation.

62379484
>>62375255

62379507
>>62375841
Anti-natalists are mentally ill and project their retardation onto others.

62379519
>>62377191
tbf I like grok more than gemini, copilot, or deepseek. Not sure yet how I think it stacks up to claude or chatgpt though.

62381795
>>62375140
how to gotted so much dollars?

62381800
>>62375128
Imagine believing 2 weeks represents absolutely anything about the viability or future trajectory of a stock. No wonder you guys get annihilated so regularly.

62381830
>>62377191
SpaceX performs 60% of the world's space launches, which is 95% of the American market. If they aren't a space company, then there isn't a space company that exists at all.

62381991
>>62381830

62382075
>>62375140

62382118

62382338
>>62382075

62382719
>>62382075

item.title: My x feed has turn into this constantly. — 17 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
My x feed has turn into this constantly.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-18 07:51 UTC

OP

My x feed has turn into this constantly.

1) some "omfg" Claude workflow that will make you 60k/month
2) retards selling courses/coaching/mentorships in how to run a gazillion automated YT/tiktok/whatever pages with screenshots of students making money
3) various clickbait: "MrBeast shares key insights", "This guy just shared to how to make 10k a month with sleep channels", "this guy just made $2,582,285 on a memecoin"

It never ends. And now with AI the effort on having to actually write something catchy isn't there. It's just constant slop with supposed cheat codes to create slop that you can monetize.

The reality is this:
-The slop on the image makes no sense, you cannot replace a Bloomberg terminal with AI because a Bloomberg terminal gets emerging constant new sources that you don't have. Of course im not saying paying for a Bloomberg terminal is going to give you an edge and make you money. But just proving my point. Also all of these "free" workflows always have a paywall somewhere.
-Course selling scammers make money from the courses, this is not news.
-Clickbaiters make money from the traffic.

No one is making money doing something new and cool. By no one I mean, 99% of people. Everything is slop. Anyone making money is doing it from some sort of loophole. It's literally everywhere. There is always something dodgy going on.

For everything cool and genuine (like John Carmack inveting Doom in 1993 and getting rich because the game was just that good and revolutionary) 99% of people that get rich are doing it scaling slop. It will only get worse as AI gets trained on AI generated slop.

And if you try to profit from this, it's just algorithm luck to get your account ranked by the system so your posts get anywhere. Everything is literally a lottery, unless your genius is so good that it eventually thrives even if you are viewthrottled by the algorithm, but that is insanely rare.

Early replies (first 3)

62382855
>>62382838
Its always funny when people for these posts. Why the fuck would someone teach other people on ways of making easy money? Its like people selling trading bots

62382857
>>62382838
You should just stop using X. I rarely even visit this site anymore. Just start building something without all that crap bombarding your mental and you'll be better off. It's all just poison for your time.

62382861
>>62382857
There is no other place to go isn't it? I have another account that I use to find interesting tickers on semis as well as Bitcoin TA takes and basically get a news feed from the walter bloomberg guy. Everyone is on x, where the fuck do you go, facebook?
The thing is, 99% is slop, there's a few good accounts, but most is just monetized slop farms.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382867
maybe unsubscribe from slop?

62382873
>>62382838
top signal

62382878
>>62382867
you can't lol
my xitter exclusively follows gook artists because pixiv sucks but it still bombards me with what is basically ads from grifters

62382884
>>62382861
I don't go anywhere else it's all pretty much the same stuff really. I guess for "socials" I just lurk some sources I like on youtube but for the most part I'm doing my own thing and everything else is just noise. These social platforms like X are all bots and junk there isn't any real substance to any of it.

62382885
>>62382867
Im not subscribed. Even if you unfollow all these accounts, they still show up because they are accounts win tons of followers. Also there's basically no good accounts except such a few ammounts that the feed is just going to get these bigger accounts anyway. Unless you limit it all to the same few accounts you follow, but this way you close yourself to finding something new worth reading.

62382892
>>62382861
Go nowhere, put the time towards slowing down and doing nothing. Honest to god I don’t even miss YouTube anymore. My only social media is Twitch Discord and this place.

62382921
>>62382857
This. X is literally like visiting India. Here is like visiting a boomer's basement to see his millennial son.

62382933
>>62382838
Who cares.

62382980
>>62382884
So you only lurk here?
How is this place any better?
I mean sure, the fact that you cannot make money here is great, you get real people, and the bots have gone down by a lot with the new captcha it seems, but this is still a slop website

62382982
>>62382892
twitch, are we fucking serious? what do you even do there but watch livesloppers
and discord, what channels? I only chat in like 2 places

62383031
>retards selling courses/coaching/mentorships
If you are a retard and ask an LLM how to make money, it will recommend selling some type of online courses.

62383080
>>62382838
>"this guy just made $2,582,285 on a memecoin"
this one is the least believable kek
Crypto is so dead

62383308
Its like how people who buy target funds are exit liquidty for other investors and nations. The people that buy it are exit liquidity for scammers. Most scammers probably got scammed multiple times before they figured it out.

62383318
>>62382892
>Twitch
This anon pays other men to fuck his wife.
